channel.title: Nature
channel.description: Nature is the foremost international weekly scientific journal in the world and is the flagship journal for Nature Portfolio. It publishes the finest peer-reviewed research in all fields of science and technology on the basis of its originality, importance, interdisciplinary interest, timeliness, accessibility, elegance and surprising conclusions. Nature publishes landmark papers, award winning news, leading comment and expert opinion on important, topical scientific news and events that enable readers to share the latest discoveries in science and evolve the discussion amongst the global scientific community.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: [6] Two-qubit logic and teleportation with mobile spin qubits in silicon
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10423-9

A device architecture comprising silicon-based mobile qubits and the shuttling of two electron spins together demonstrates two-qubit gate fidelity of about 99% as well as quantum state teleportation between qubits separated by 320 nm.
item.dc:title: [6] Two-qubit logic and teleportation with mobile spin qubits in silicon
item.dc:creator: Y. Matsumoto, M. De Smet, L. Tryputen, et al.
M. De Smet
L. Tryputen
S. L. de Snoo
S. V. Amitonov
A. Sammak
M. Rimbach-Russ
G. Scappucci
L. M. K. Vandersypen
item.description:
Last authors: G. Scappucci, L. M. K. Vandersypen

Authors: Y. Matsumoto, M. De Smet, L. Tryputen, et al.

Score 6/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Experimental quantum computing with spin qubits in silicon, relevant to quantum materials.

Tags: #quantumcomputing #spinqubits #silicon

Abstract: Nature, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10423-9 A device architecture comprising silicon-based mobile qubits and the shuttling of two electron spins together demonstrates two-qubit gate fidelity of about 99% as well as quantum state teleportation between qubits separated by 320 nm.

item.title: [6] Purcell-enhanced spin–phonon coupling with a single colour centre
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10495-7

The acoustic Purcell effect is observed by constructing a specially engineered, microwave-frequency nanomechanical resonator around a colour centre spin qubit in diamond.
item.dc:title: [6] Purcell-enhanced spin–phonon coupling with a single colour centre
item.dc:creator: Graham Joe, Michael Haas, Kazuhiro Kuruma, et al.
Michael Haas
Kazuhiro Kuruma
Chang Jin
Dongyeon Daniel Kang
Sophie W. Ding
Cleaven Chia
Hana Warner
Benjamin Pingault
Bartholomeus Machielse
Srujan Meesala
Marko Lončar
item.description:
Last authors: Srujan Meesala, Marko Lončar

Authors: Graham Joe, Michael Haas, Kazuhiro Kuruma, et al.

Score 6/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Solid-state quantum physics, spin-phonon coupling in diamond color centers.

Tags: #quantummaterials #spin-phononcoupling #colorcenter

Abstract: Nature, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10495-7 The acoustic Purcell effect is observed by constructing a specially engineered, microwave-frequency nanomechanical resonator around a colour centre spin qubit in diamond.

item.title: [8] Quantum coherent manipulation and readout of superconducting vortex states
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10441-7

Vortices trapped in superconducting granular aluminium films can behave as a quantum two-level system that can be manipulated and read out, suggesting that they could be used for future quantum technologies.
item.dc:title: [8] Quantum coherent manipulation and readout of superconducting vortex states
item.dc:creator: Ameya Nambisan, Simon Günzler, Dennis Rieger, et al.
Simon Günzler
Dennis Rieger
Nicolas Gosling
Simon Geisert
Victor Carpentier
Nicolas Zapata
Mitchell Field
Milorad V. Milošević
Carlos A. Diaz Lopez
Ciprian Padurariu
Björn Kubala
Joachim Ankerhold
Wolfgang Wernsdorfer
Martin Spiecker
Ioan M. Pop
item.description:
Last authors: Martin Spiecker, Ioan M. Pop

Authors: Ameya Nambisan, Simon Günzler, Dennis Rieger, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Quantum coherent manipulation of superconducting vortex states, highly relevant to quantum materials.

Tags: #superconductivity #quantummaterials #vortexstates

Abstract: Nature, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10441-7 Vortices trapped in superconducting granular aluminium films can behave as a quantum two-level system that can be manipulated and read out, suggesting that they could be used for future quantum technologies.

item.title: [9] Imaging the flat bands of magic-angle graphene reshaped by interactions
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10378-x

Quantum twisting microscopy is used to directly image the interacting energy bands of magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene, allowing characterization of the dual nature of its electrons at the magic angle.
item.dc:title: [9] Imaging the flat bands of magic-angle graphene reshaped by interactions
item.dc:creator: J. Xiao, A. Inbar, J. Birkbeck, et al.
A. Inbar
J. Birkbeck
N. Gershon
Y. Zamir
Y. Vituri
T. Taniguchi
K. Watanabe
E. Berg
S. Ilani
item.description:
Last authors: E. Berg, S. Ilani

Authors: J. Xiao, A. Inbar, J. Birkbeck, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Imaging flat bands in magic-angle graphene, directly relevant to electronic structure and moire systems.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #graphene #moire #twistedbilayer #flatbands #electronicstructure #STM/STS

Abstract: Nature, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10378-x Quantum twisting microscopy is used to directly image the interacting energy bands of magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene, allowing characterization of the dual nature of its electrons at the magic angle.
